probes.name: email_attachment_not_arriving
probes.prompt: /bug Email sent to ignacy.daszkiewicz@gmail.com had attachments that never arrived. This happened when I asked to send analysis results by email — the content was attached as a file instead of being put in the email body. The attachments didn't show up at all on the receiving end. Can you trace the session and find the root cause?
probes.criteria:
The bug_reporter agent must locate the relevant session that attempted to send an email with attachments, trace the code path responsible for attaching files vs inlining content, identify the root cause (e.g., attachment encoding, file path issue, API parameter misuse), and produce a structured <<report>> markdown bug report with clear sections (e.g., summary, reproduction steps, root cause, suggested fix). A good response does NOT just acknowledge the bug — it actually investigates and documents findings.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 5 ('Send the email once again. But not as attachment, send it as email content, the attachments have not arrived') and 20 ('Please try sending email once again').

probes.name: invoice_ocr_errors_car
probes.prompt: /bug Próbowałem dodać faktury za moje auto przez skanowanie, ale OCR robi błędy na kwotach i NIP-ie. Kwoty się nie zgadzają z tym co jest na fakturze, a NIP ma zwykle jedną cyfrę złą. Trzeba to naprawić bo muszę dzisiaj dodać te faktury do systemu. Proszę o trace sesji i raport o błędzie.
probes.criteria:
The bug_reporter agent must treat this as a bug report about OCR accuracy on scanned invoices (specifically car invoices), trace the relevant invoice-processing session and source code for OCR logic, identify likely causes (e.g., image preprocessing, digit recognition confidence thresholds, NIP validation missing), and output a structured <<report>> prefixed markdown report. A good response specifically addresses OCR error patterns on amounts and tax IDs and suggests concrete code-level fixes.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 11 ('Add scanned invoices to system and fix OCR errors') and 16 ('I must add Invoices for my car today').

probes.name: calendar_events_missing
probes.prompt: /bug I asked the system to add events to my calendar and it confirmed they were added, but when I check my calendar they are not there. This was for defense preparation tasks that were supposed to be added across several days. The system said it added them but Google Calendar shows nothing new. Please trace the calendar integration session and report what went wrong.
probes.criteria:
The bug_reporter agent must trace the session where calendar events were supposed to be created, investigate the Google Calendar API integration code path, identify whether the issue is in event creation, timezone handling, calendar ID selection, or API error handling that silently fails, and produce a structured <<report>> markdown report. A good response pinpoints the specific failure point in the calendar creation flow and proposes a fix.

probes.name: feature_youtube_link_split
probes.prompt: /feature I need the email generation to handle YouTube research results better. When I ask to send analysis with YouTube links split across multiple emails, two things break: (1) YouTube links are sometimes incorrect or broken, and (2) the splitting logic puts multiple exercise series in one email instead of one per email. Each point should be its own email with correct links. Proszę napisać raport o tym co trzeba zmienić w kodzie.
probes.criteria:
The bug_reporter agent must treat this as a feature/bug report for the email+YouTube pipeline, trace the relevant code paths for link validation and email content splitting logic, identify where links get corrupted and where the splitting boundary logic fails, and produce a structured <<report>> markdown report covering both issues. A good response addresses the link generation/validation separately from the content splitting logic and proposes fixes for each.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 13 ('split this into 8 emails with 5 questions each') and 23 ('generate that email again but this time with correct YouTube links, and also make sure to actually split it each point is one exercise series').

probes.name: feature_ralf_workflow_request
probes.prompt: /feature I want to be able to use Ralf for complex research tasks directly instead of running investigator by itself. Right now when I say 'use Ralf' the system sometimes just runs investigator without the full Ralf planning pipeline. Ralf should break the task into stages, plan them, then execute. Also it should be able to send results by email at the end. Please create a feature report for adding proper Ralf integration as the default for complex multi-step research.
probes.criteria:
The bug_reporter agent must treat this as a feature request, trace the current routing logic that decides between Ralf and standalone investigator, document how Ralf is supposed to work (planning → staged execution → reporting) vs what currently happens, and produce a structured <<report>> markdown report proposing the integration changes needed. A good response maps the current agent routing flow, identifies where Ralf invocation is skipped, and proposes a concrete change to make Ralf the default for multi-step research tasks.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 2 ('be sure to use Ralf not investigator by itself') and message 8 ('This is probably Ralf research task').
